subject: Ecology
title: Honeybees
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Honeybees.

hook.text: Honeybees are the most managed pollinator on Earth—and also one of the most misunderstood.

buildup.text: A single colony visits millions of flowers per day, collecting nectar and pollen for the hive.

discovery.text: Bees communicate flower locations through the waggle dance—a map encoded in movement.

twist.text: Honeybees are not native to North America. They were brought by European colonists in the 1600s.

climax.text: Relying too heavily on one species for pollination is risky—wild pollinators provide a safety net.

punchline.text: Honeybees are important. They're not the only ones.

quiz.question: How do honeybees communicate flower locations?
